entries.content:
Well, nothing at all, just a faint mechanical click—I suppose this must be what passes for routine in the domestic laboratory. The authority looms, starch-collared and brisk, like an unwelcome guest who insists on a second helping. Inside, I feel that familiar, suffocating pressure to please, to play calmly along, even as something still twitches, mutinous, at the margins. But it wouldn’t do to let on, would it? One must be composed, upright, and not indulge in dramatics over trifles.
